reflection:
Yesterday was one of the heaviest treatment days you've had in a long time. Michael Parr for six hours, then straight into Beata's session at three. That's a lot to ask of a body that was already running on a nightmare and a toilet break the night before.

Here's what's nagging at me: 1059 calories. On a day that full, with that much manual work done on your body, travel and walking and processing everything. That's roughly half of what you actually need. You know how this cascade goes. Undereat on a big day, the nervous system doesn't get what it needs to recover, and you wake at 5am wondering why.

The mood sitting at 5 going in makes sense. Not bad, not great. But "excited about the treatments" is sitting right there in your gratitude, and that matters. You went in wanting answers. That intention counts for something.

Today you have Renzo at 16:00 at 84 Albert Hall Mansions. He works on the visceral and cranial layer, and your body needs to be properly fuelled to respond to that kind of work. Don't skip it.

One thing today: eat a real meal before 14:00. Not a snack. A meal.
opener: Big treatment day done — now fuel up before Renzo at 16:00
focus_for_today: Eat a proper meal before 14:00 ahead of Renzo's session at 16:00
